mail_settings.title: Outbound mail (panel notifications)
mail_settings.subtitle: Password resets and system mail. Manage here without SSH or editing .env.
mail_settings.hint: The install script defaults to local Postfix + sendmail. Choose SMTP for SendGrid, corporate relay, etc.
mail_settings.password_unchanged_hint: Leave empty to keep the current password
mail_settings.test_hint: Test uses saved settings; click Save first if you changed the form.
mail_settings.not_persisted_warning: No mail settings are saved in the panel yet. Click Save to persist them before testing or sending real mail (filling the form alone is not enough).
mail_settings.test_disabled_log: The log driver does not send real email; choose sendmail or SMTP and save to run a test.

stack.title: Server packages (PHP & mail)
stack.subtitle: Whitelisted bundles the engine can install via sudo on Debian/Ubuntu.
stack.warning: This runs apt installs; use backups and a maintenance window in production. After Postfix/Dovecot you still need DNS, firewall, and app (.env SMTP) configuration.
stack.empty_hint: If the list is empty, the engine may be down or the API key may not match.

terminal.title: SSH terminal
terminal.subtitle: Root login shell on the server (via sudo). Admin only; needs Nginx /engine-ws/ proxy and ENGINE_API_SECRET matching engine jwt_secret.
terminal.warning: Full root access: a wrong command can break the server. Only trusted admins should use this. Set PANELSAR_TERMINAL_NO_ROOT=1 on the engine service to fall back to the engine user shell.
terminal.use_root_hint: When off, an interactive shell runs as the engine user (www-data). Stored in the signed JWT; no engine restart needed.
terminal.settings_saved_reconnect: Saved. Click Reconnect for a new session.
terminal.warning_limited: Limited privileges (www-data): site files and allowed paths only. Enable root or use SSH for full system administration.

license.subtitle: License key and engine validation
license.engine_skip: No local key; engine call skipped
license.env_hint: Set LICENSE_KEY in .env on production; do not store keys in the browser.
license.try_key_hint: For testing only; keep production keys on the server.

dashboard.detail_cpu_intro: CPU summary and top processes by usage.
dashboard.detail_ram_intro: RAM and swap summary; top memory consumers.
dashboard.detail_disk_intro: Panel disk summary and fullest mount points.
dashboard.top3_disk_hint: Based on system mount points; panel root may differ.
dashboard.system_admin_only: Server resource summary is shown to admins via the engine only.
dashboard.system_engine_hint: If the engine is down, stats will not appear. Check the engine and ENGINE_INTERNAL_KEY.

domains.title: Domain Management
domains.name: Domain Name
domains.ssl_revoke_confirm: Remove SSL certificate for this domain?
domains.ssl_progress_error: SSL step failed — see message above.
domains.delete_warning: This removes the site from the web server, site files, linked databases (MySQL/PostgreSQL when provisioned through the panel), mailboxes, DNS records in the panel, FTP accounts, backup records, and the domain row. This cannot be undone.
domains.confirm_php_change: PHP will change to {{php}}. Are you sure?
domains.confirm_server_change: Web server will change to {{server}}. Are you sure?
domains.confirm_status_change: Site status will change to {{status}}. Continue?
domains.confirm_ssl_issue: Let's Encrypt SSL setup will start. Validation may take a few minutes. Continue?

databases.title: Database Management
databases.name: Database Name
databases.ui_url_missing: Web UI URL is not configured on the server:
databases.no_web_ui_for_type: No web UI is configured for this database type.
databases.grant_host_hint: localhost = same server; % = any host (use carefully)
databases.password_rotated: Password rotated — save the new password

common.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this?

files.subtitle: Full path, drag-and-drop, double-click edit, and content search
files.name: Name
files.folder_dblclick_hint: Folders: single-click to select, double-click to open.
files.list_error: Could not load file list (engine or site path).
files.drop_zone_hint: Drag files onto this list or use «Upload file» above for multi-select.
files.quick_new_file: New file (with extension, e.g. index.html) — opens in the editor when editable
files.invalid_filename: Invalid name: use letters, digits, dot, hyphen, underscore only; no path separators.
files.file_exists: A file with this name already exists.
files.unsaved_close: You have unsaved changes. Close anyway?
files.cannot_edit: This file type cannot be opened in the editor
files.risky_readonly: This file type is read-only for security reasons.
files.help_intro: In this file manager, the top folder («Site root» in the breadcrumb) is your site’s document root. On most installs this matches **public_html** on the server; put publicly visible files here or in subfolders.
files.help_upload_hint: Drag-and-drop or **Upload** places files into the current folder. For WordPress you usually see wp-content, wp-admin, etc. under this root.
files.perm_backup_na: Not available in the panel yet.
files.recycle_na: Deletes are permanent; there is no recycle bin.
files.calc_folder_na: Folder size needs a server-side scan; not available yet.

backups.subtitle: Full / files / database backups
backups.restore_unavailable: No engine backup id for this row (take a new backup)

email.subtitle: Mailboxes for your domains. Reading mail uses IMAP — this page is not webmail; use a mail app or installed web UI.
email.guide_intro: Mailboxes you create here usually map to a real mailbox on the server. The panel does not show an inbox like Roundcube; use a desktop/mobile client or a separately installed webmail app.
email.card_incoming_body: You typically connect with IMAP or POP3. The server hostname is often mail.yourdomain.com or your host’s server name — confirm with your provider.
email.card_outgoing_body: Sending uses SMTP (often port 587 with TLS or 465 with SSL). Your panel login password and mailbox password are different things.
email.card_panel_mail_body: Password resets and system email use the admin-configured outbound mail (SMTP/sendmail); that is separate from these mailboxes.
email.ports_hint: Common: IMAP 993 (SSL), SMTP 587 (STARTTLS) — varies by server.
email.dns_hint: For reliable delivery, ensure MX, SPF, and ideally DKIM DNS records are correct (edit on the DNS page).
email.new_password_hint: Leave empty to keep the current password.
email.password_once: We show the new password only once — store it safely.
email.empty_mailboxes: No mailboxes for this domain yet. Create one to get started.
email.webmail_note_body: This panel does not display your inbox. Use Outlook, Apple Mail, Thunderbird, or a server-installed webmail such as Roundcube/SOGo; use the address and password you set here.

cron.subtitle: Presets, custom cron expression, and quota summary
cron.info_fields: Format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week — five fields (same order as Linux crontab).
cron.info_timezone: Times use the panel app timezone: {{tz}} (align with your server/engine).
cron.info_engine: Jobs are stored via the engine; actual execution may still require a server-side cron runner.
cron.info_paths: Use absolute paths in commands (php, wget, your site directory).
cron.examples_body:
# Laravel (change path to your site)
0 * * * *  →  /usr/bin/php /var/www/site/public_html/artisan schedule:run

# WordPress ping
*/15 * * * *  →  wget -q -O - https://example.com/wp-cron.php?doing_wp_cron

# PHP script
0 2 * * *  →  /usr/bin/php /var/www/site/public_html/scripts/backup.php
cron.empty_hint: No tasks yet. Add one above or check your package quota.
cron.custom_fields_hint: The fields below keep the single line in sync.

tools.title: Site tools
tools.subtitle: Composer / npm in the site document root (engine: manage_site_tools)

system.title: System
system.subtitle: Server resources, services, and web server
